Football.title: Fabian Hurzeler insists Arsenal star should’ve been sent off and demands Premier League intervention
Football.content:
Fabian Hurzeler insists Arsenal star should’ve been sent off and demands Premier League intervention

Fabian Hurzeler accused Arsenal of time-wasting in their victory over Brighton, insisting David Raya should have been sent off for this.

The Seagulls boss was left furious by the Gunners' tactics as they secured a gritty 1-0 win at the Amex Stadium to open up a seven-point lead at the Premier League summit.

5 Hurzeler was left incensed by Arsenal's tactics, just 24 hours after he had hinted at time-wasting from them Credit: AFP

After Bukayo Saka's ninth-minute goal, Mikel Arteta's side put in a resolute display to grind out the three points on the south cost.



It was far from a pretty performance from the league leaders, who were put under huge pressure by Brighton as they chased an equaliser.

As Arsenal tried to take the sting out of the game, the home crowd and Hurzeler were left infuriated by their perceived time-wasting.

Just before the hour mark, David Raya saved from Georginio Rutter, before remaining on the floor clutching his shoulder and later receiving treatment.

This left Hurzeler incensed on the touchline, who viewed it as a deliberate delay tactic, though the Spaniard was not booked.



Hurzeler hits out

In the build-up to the game, the German had called on the Premier League to crack down on time-wasting at corners, and cited Arsenal as beneficiaries of the current rules.

And following his side's defeat, Hurzeler once again took aim at the Gunners' tactics, as he blasted them for slowing the game down.



In an apparent dig at the visitors' style of play, he began: "I think we tried to play football and that's what we are standing for.

“And of course it's not easy to always get into a rhythm, but still we had a lot of chances so there was enough opportunity to take a win and therefore it's not easy to get into rhythm with an opponent who tries to only to waste time.



“Definitely we can do better in the final third, but overall we try to play football and we try to find solutions."

5 Saka scored the only goal of the game as Arsenal boosted their Premier League title bid Credit: AFP

'The Premier League might intervene'

Hurzeler then claimed Raya should have been booked twice for deliberate slow play, which would have seen him sent off.

The 33-year-old once again called on the Premier League to intervene, insisting clearer rules are needed to outlaw such tactics.

He continued: "I think it's difficult, but we just need clearer rules, and normally the goalkeeper should be sent off with two yellow cards for time-wasting, but you can't do it and therefore if you don't have clear rules, I think it's difficult for the referee.



“But I made my point before it and like I just mentioned we have to find a solution.

“The Premier League has to find a solution because I don't know how you see it but for me it doesn't make it fun to see these kind of games."

Hurzeler wasn't the only person unimpressed by Arsenal's tactics in the clash, with former Newcastle, Crystal Palace and West Ham boss Alan Pardew claiming their style of play will take away from their Premier League title triumph if they win the league this season

5 Arsenal kept a 13th clean sheet of the season against Brighton Credit: Getty

5 Hurzeler certainly didn't like how the Gunners went about their win Credit: AFP



Pardew claimed the Gunners will have an 'asterisk next to their name' should they triumph, which Hurzeler was asked about in his post-match interview.

The Seagulls boss replied: "I think everyone should judge on their own.

“I don't want to waste too much time talking about Arsenal because I made my point and therefore let's see how the next nine games are going.

“The Premier League might intervene on what the next referee tries to do against this time-wasting and then we see what happens. But we have to focus on us."

Arteta plays down Hurzeler outrage

With Hurzeler visibly frustrated on the touchline, Arsenal boss Arteta was asked about the tension between himself and his opposite number.



Playing this down, he insisted: "Not from my side, no… it is what it is."

5 Arteta's Arsenal are chasing a first Premier League title since 2004 Credit: Getty

The Spaniard was then quizzed on Hurzeler's calls for the Premier League to change their rules, and appeared to suggest there would be little long-term benefit to this.

On a potential rule change, Arteta said: "No, they [the Premier League] would have to react every week. So, no point."

Arteta and Arsenal enjoyed a hugely positive night at the Amex, as they clinched three points while title rivals City were held to a 2-2 draw at home by Nottingham Forest.



The results mean the title race is firmly back in the Gunners' hands despite City's game in hand.

Football.title: Arsenal will have asterisk next to their name if they win the Premier League, there is nothing beautiful about them
Football.content:
Arsenal will have asterisk next to their name if they win the Premier League, there is nothing beautiful about them

Arsenal’s Premier League performances this season will take the shine off a possible title triumph.

That is the view of Alan Pardew after the Gunners extended their lead over Manchester City to seven points.

5 Arsenal should have an asterisk next to their Premier League title if they go on to win it, says Pardew Credit: Getty

Mikel Arteta's side clinched a 1-0 win at Brighton on Wednesday night thanks to Bukayo Saka's first half strike.



Meanwhile, title rivals City were held to a 2-2 draw at home by Nottingham Forest despite twice taking the lead at the Etihad Stadium.

The results mean the title race is firmly back in Arsenal's hands despite City's game in hand.

Even if Pep Guardiola's side win that match as well as their clash with Arsenal on April 19, they would still be a point adrift unless the Gunners drop further points.

However, Pardew believes there must still be an asterisk on any potential Premier League title for the north London club.



The former Newcastle United manager praised their 'professional' display against Brighton but believes there is 'nothing beautiful about them'.

'Asterisk next to their name': Pardew

Pardew assessed Arsenal's title credentials live on commentary for talkSPORT during Wednesday's action, explaining: "When I'm looking at them as my football manager's head on, they're so professional.



"They do every bit of gamesmanship to get it over the line. It's only a goal, isn't it?

"But really and truly, Brighton, although they have definitely upped the tempo and looked better second half, they've not really hurt Arsenal.



"And at the moment, it looks like Arsenal are going to see it through.

5 Pardew claimed there is 'nothing beautiful' about Arsenal after their win over Brighton Credit: Getty

"There'll be an asterisk next to their name because you've only got to look at them really in these type of performances and say, it's just a functional display.

"It's a professional, functional display. They're good, they're strong, they're powerful. But there is nothing beautiful about them. That's the truth."

Stats tell the story

Following their narrow win over the Seagulls, Arsenal have now won ten Premier League matches by a single goal this season.



It is their most at this stage of a campaign since the 2011/12 season, where they had the same amount.

Meanwhile, the only time they had more one-goal victories after 30 matches was during their invincible 2003/04 title win with 12.

Their 14 clean sheets so far this term is also their most at this point in a league season for 20 years.

5 Arteta may be concerned by his side's passing and shooting stats despite their victory... or not Credit: Getty

However, there were also less impressive takeaways from Arsenal's latest triumph.



The Gunners mustered just seven shots on goal against Brighton, with only their five against Chelsea in the Carabao Cup last month representing fewer.

It was also a night to forget in the passing department for the visitors as they recorded a pass accuracy of just 70.9 per cent.

The figure represents their lowest completion rate for over five years, dating back to a 5-0 thrashing against City in August 2021.

Pardew's comments come after Arsenal were once again labelled 'Set Piece FC' by rival fans following their win over Chelsea at the weekend.

The Gunners scored twice from corners to see off their London rivals on Sunday.



Arsenal legend fumes at 'Set Piece FC' label given to Mikel Arteta's side

5 City's draw with Forest has handed the initiative firmly back to Arsenal in the title race Credit: Getty

Those goals took their tally from set plays in all competitions this season to 31, with 24 coming from corners.

Meanwhile, Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler called out Arsenal's time-wasting at corners ahead of their meeting.

Having urged the Premier League to outline 'clear rules', he reiterated his point after his side's defeat to the league leaders.

"I think we tried to play football and that's what we are standing for,” he said.



"And of course it's not easy to always get into a rhythm, but still we had a lot of chances so there was enough opportunity to take a win and therefore it's not easy to get into rhythm with an opponent who tries to only to waste time.

"Definitely we can do better in the final third, but overall we try to play football and we try to find solutions.

5 Hurzeler was once again angered by Arsenal's time-wasting during Wednesday's clash Credit: AFP

"I think it's difficult, but we need just more clearer rules and normally the goalkeeper should should be sent off with two yellow cards for time-wasting, but you can't do it and therefore if you don't have clear rules, I think it's difficult for for the referee.

"But I made my point before it and like I just mentioned we have to find a solution.



"The Premier League has to find a solution because I don't know how you see it but for me it doesn't make fun to see these kind of games."

When Pardew’s comments were put to him he told talkSPORT: "I think everyone should judge on their own.

"I don't want to waste too much time talking about Arsenal because I made my point and therefore let's see how how the next nine games are going.

"The Premier League might intervene on what the next referee tries to do against this time-wasting and then we see what happens. But we have to focus on us."

Arsenal are aiming to win their first league title for 22 years after a series of disappointments in recent seasons.



The Gunners have finished second in each of the last three seasons, with two of those seeing them miss out on the title to City.

News.title: Illegal migrant families to be paid up to £40k to leave UK within a week as Shabana Mahmood reveals migration overhaul
News.content:
ILLEGAL migrant families will be offered a whopping £40,000 to get out of Britain voluntarily.

Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood announced today that under a pilot scheme, 150 arrivals will be able to claim £10,000 per person – including kids – to leave within a week.





Outlining her immigration overhaul in Westminster, Ms Mahmood said: “Failed asylum seekers will now be offered an increased incentive payment of £10,000 per person, and up to a maximum of £40,000 per family.

“To put that in important context, today, a family of three asylum hotel accommodation costs up to £158,000 per year.

“Should these incentives prove effective, they will represent a significant saving to the taxpayer.

“Where a voluntary removal is refused, we will escalate to an enforced removal for those who can be returned to their safe home country.”



Ms Mahmood today threw down the gauntlet to squeamish Labour MPs as she unleashed her hardline migration crackdown.

She warned lefties in her party that “at the same time as showing generosity, we must wrest control over migration back from the smugglers and restore order and control at our border”.

It came as 100 Labour MPs signed a letter to the PM slamming proposals to crackdown on both lawful and illegal migration.

Referencing demands for Sir Keir to push his party leftward, the Home Secretary admitted that “we meet at a difficult time for my party”.



But she slapped down pleas to abandon her migration reforms.

Ms Mahmood said: “Of course, we should be more Labour. The real question is, what does more Labour mean, because, in my view, more Labour doesn’t mean more Green, just like more Labour doesn’t mean more Reform.

“More Labour means reconnecting with who we are, who we represent, and what we believe.”

The Home Secretary insisted her sweeping changes demonstrate both “compassion and control” and sits between the “extremes” of the Greens and Reform.

She added: “They are no invitation to the fairy tale of open borders as Zack Polanski’s revolutionary green party demands, and neither are they the nightmare of Nigel Farage pulling up the drawbridge and shutting out the world, narrowing our proud patriotism into a crude ethno nationalism.



“Between those two extremes there is ample space for a migration system that is fair, and where it needs to be firm.”

She launched a pilot scheme offering failed asylum-seeker families up to £40,000 each to leave Britain within seven days.

Those who refuse the offer risked being detained and forcibly removed instead.

Ministers said the payouts could save taxpayers millions, as some families cost up to £158,000 a year in hotels and support.

Under the new rules, migrants will also have to speak English to A-level standard before they can settle permanently in Britain from next year.



Refugees granted protection no longer receive automatic long-term status, but are instead placed on temporary 30-month protection visas subject to review.

Asylum seekers who brake the law, work illegally or can support themselves lose taxpayer-funded accommodation and cash support.

Officials said the measures will help bring down the eye-watering £4 billion asylum support bill.

The Home Secretary declared the changes were needed to restore “order and control” to Britain’s borders.

She said the reforms struck a balance between “Farage’s nightmare pulling up the drawbridge and shutting out the world” and “Polanski’s fairy tale of open borders”.



In the speech, she also insisted restoring control of migration was “an embodiment” of Labour values.

But the package is already sparking anger among left-wing Labour MPs who warned the government risked going too far.

Some of the most controversial reforms to the settlement system are expected to appear in primary legislation in the King’s Speech in May under her proposed “earned settlement model”.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

This will see the normal route to settlement double from five years to ten for most migrants, with faster tracks for those who made a strong contribution to Britain’s economy and society.

Under the plans, skilled frontline public service workers such as doctors and nurses will still qualify after five years.

News.title: Elite Ukrainian drone hunters to be deployed to help Gulf fend off Iran drone onslaught as Brits frantically try to flee
News.content:
UKRAINE’S drone experts will help Gulf states defend against the barrage of attacks from Iran.

President Volodymyr Zelensky offered his help to partners in the Middle East, even as Mad Vlad continues to pummel Ukraine with drone attacks.





Zelensky will send his elite drone hunters to Gulf nations to share their experience of shooting down enemy aircraft.

The Ukrainian president said: “Our military possesses the necessary capabilities.

“Ukrainian experts will work on site, and the teams are already discussing this.”



The deadly drones being launched at US bases and other targets across the Middle East are the same as those that Putin has relentlessly aimed at Ukrainians.

No country in the world has better experience at striking down these drones.

Zelensky revealed: “And we are ready to help protect lives, protect civilians, and support real efforts to stabilise the situation and restore, in particular, safe shipping in the region.

“Ukraine is conducting consultations with partners in Europe and the United States, as well as with Iran‘s neighbouring countries.”



He has spoken to the leaders of the UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, and Jordan – all of which are facing daily onslaughts of drones as Iran wreaks its revenge for US-Israeli operation Epic Fury.

Follow The Sun’s live blog for the latest updates on Iran

The Ukrainian president added: “There will be further talks with Kuwait and other countries in the region.

“All of them face serious challenges and speak openly about them.

“Iranian attack drones – the same Shahed drones that have been attacking our cities and villages, our Ukrainian infrastructure for years during this war.”



It comes as Ukraine asked the US about potentially pushing back or changing the location of the next round of peace talks with Russia.

The next talks mediated by the US were planned for 5 March – 9 March, but chaos in the Middle East could disrupt them.



Zelensky said: “The Iranian regime, which is trying to survive at all costs, poses obvious threats to all states in the region, as well as to global stability.

It comes as…

“No state neighbouring Iran can feel safe. The Iranian regime has not yet demonstrated any real intention of engaging in honest diplomacy or making fundamental changes.”

He ordered his military and intelligence commanders “to present options for support to relevant states and assistance in such a way that it does not weaken our own defence here in Ukraine”.

He called for backing from leading Western states to assist this effort.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

It comes as the first repatriation flight slated to save British tourists stranded in the Middle East was grounded this morning.

The flight was supposed to depart Muscat, Oman, at 11pm local time (7pm UK time) on Wednesday – but instead, after enduring agonising waits, Brits were sent back to their hotels.



﻿

News.title: Teenager and man in his 20s arrested over viral ‘school wars’ craze as cops warn parents over violent social media trend
News.content:
SCHOOL WARS Teenager and man in his 20s arrested over viral ‘school wars’ craze as cops warn parents over violent social media trend

A TEENAGE boy and a man his 20s have been arrested in connection with the viral “school wars” trend which urged children to be violent.

The posts pitted pupils from rival schools against each other and told students as young as 11 to get weapons and be ready for “war”.



The Metropolitan Police arrested the boy, 15, and the man on Sunday on suspicion of encouraging or assisting in a crime, they have both been released on bail.



Posts rapidly appeared last month across several platforms dividing schools into team red and team blue inspired by the notorious Bloods and Crips gangs.

The images showed masked figures or household items that could be used as weapons and sparked concerns for parents across London.

Boroughs in the capital were pitted against each other as Year 7 to Year 11 schools were divided into the two camps.

Viral TikTok posts from anonymous accounts saw pupils being urged to bring compasses and combs ready to fight each other.



Parents were advised to collect children from the school gates and many expressed fears on Facebook.

A spokesperson for the force said: “On Sunday, 1 March at approximately 18:45hrs, Met officers arrested two individuals in Central East London in connection with a recent trend of social media posts.”

They added: “A strong police presence remains around schools, particularly at the end of the school day.

Local officers continue to work alongside local authority partners, school leaders and parents, whose joint robust response has been vital in protecting young people across the last week.”

News.title: BBC to ‘SCRAP licence fee but Brits will be hit with council tax hike instead’ even if you don’t have a TV
News.content:
THE BBC is reportedly considering scrapping their licence fee but Brits will be hit with a council tax hike instead – even if they don’t own a TV.

The fee is due to rise to £180 in April – but the corporation is now expected to propose a major shake-up with a new “fair and sustainable” universal funding model.



Currently, viewers of live TV or anything on iPlayer must pay the licence fee but it’s not required for listening to radio or podcasts, or website access.



The licence fee model as we know it today was introduced in 1946, costing just £2, and in 1971 radios were exempted.

But unpaid fees cost the BBC more than £1billion last year, it was revealed.

Now, the Beeb will look to overhaul the charge as it seeks to combat rising levels of evasion and non-payment, reports The i Paper.

In its response to a Government Green Paper on the future of the broadcaster, the BBC will rule out putting adverts on its main channels or hiding programmes behind a Netflix-style subscription paywall, according to the outlet.



Instead, bosses are expected to push for a new “universal funding model fit for the future” to replace the licence fee – which brought in £3.8billion last year.

Insiders say the BBC has been exploring alternatives to the current model – including scrapping controversial house visits from licence fee inspectors and the “threatening” letters sent to suspected non-payers.

And one option under consideration is a household levy that could be collected through council tax.

That would mean the charge could apply to far more homes than the 24million households who currently pay the licence fee.



Under the idea, payments could be adjusted depending on income, meaning lower earners would pay less.

Some groups – such as students – could receive discounts or even free access, it is understood.

The new system would reportedly be designed to be “sufficient, sustainable and fair”, while still raising enough cash to support universal public service broadcasting.

An insider told The i: “The BBC recognises we can’t keep asking households across the board to pay an increased licence fee every year which doesn’t keep up with the inflationary costs of programme-making.

“Subscription is out because the BBC has to be a universal public service broadcaster.



“We need a fair, progressive system that means the BBC can compete against streamers with secure funding for decades ahead.”

How to watch TV legally without paying for a licence YOU can legally use the following services without a TV Licence as long as you aren’t using them to watch or stream live TV: On demand TV – such as catch-up TV and on demand previews, which are available through services including ITV Player, All 4, My5, BT Vision/BT TV, Virgin Media, Sky Go, Now TV, Apple TV, Chromecast, Roku and Amazon Fire TV. You can’t watch or download programmes on BBC iPlayer without a TV licence.

– such as catch-up TV and on demand previews, which are available through services including ITV Player, All 4, My5, BT Vision/BT TV, Virgin Media, Sky Go, Now TV, Apple TV, Chromecast, Roku and Amazon Fire TV. You can’t watch or download programmes on BBC iPlayer without a TV licence. On demand movies – from services such as Sky, Virgin Media, BT Vision, Netflix and Amazon Instant Video.

– from services such as Sky, Virgin Media, BT Vision, Netflix and Amazon Instant Video. Recorded films and programmes – either via DVD or Blu-ray, or downloaded from the internet.

– either via DVD or Blu-ray, or downloaded from the internet. YouTube – Video clips that aren’t live through services such as YouTube.

The proposal is similar to Germany’s system, where households pay a “digital public service media” tax of €18.36 (£16) a month whether they watch or listen to the country’s equivalent of the BBC or not.

Any overhaul would not come in before 2028, when the BBC’s next Royal Charter – which sets out how the corporation is run and funded – begins.

But the plan faces a major hurdle as ministers would have to back it.

Downing Street may be wary of introducing anything that critics could brand a new “TV tax”.



The BBC’s charter proposals also suggest letting viewers have a direct say in decisions through so-called “citizen assemblies”.

The broadcaster lost more than £1 billion last year after a record number of households refused to pay the licence fee, according to the Public Accounts Committee.

It is currently working on iPlayer tracking tech to sniff out non-payers, according to reports.

From April 1, the annual fee for a colour TV will jump to £180 – up from £174.50.

Government officials confirmed the news last month and explained that the price of a standard colour licence will increase by £5.50 over the current rate.



For families already feeling the pinch, this boils down to an extra 46p being snatched from their bank accounts every single month.

The Sun previously reported how the BBC could scrap the TV licence fee for millions and put shows like Line of Duty and Top Gear behind a paywall.

It is understood other options considered include finding a different form of public funding to support the World Service.

The submission could also see BBC bosses suggest to Downing Street that production arm BBC Studios could take on more debt while it expands.

The broadcaster is yet to finalise its proposals but reports suggest key will be urging ministers not to favour a “status quo” licence fee model.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

The government has suggested a Netflix-style subscription or advertising – though it’s thought Beeb bosses are not keen on either.

Ministers have, however, ruled out a new tax model which would see the fee collected like a utility bill.

News.title: How scared Dubai influencers risk up to 5 years in jail and £200k fines for revealing true scale of Iran drone terror
News.content:
REPRESSIVE rules in the United Arab Emirates have silenced Brit influencers from sharing their true feelings about terrifying Iranian drone attacks on Dubai.

Residents have been threatened with legal action for “circulating rumours or unverified news“, while authorities appeared to wrongly suggest that videos of a fire at the popular Fairmont hotel – later confirmed to have been caused by drone debris – was old footage from a previous incident.





Dubai‘s media office posted a chilling warning as missiles soared through the skies above the Gulf city whose popularity was built on its reputation as a low-tax safe haven for wealthy expats.

Several Brit influencers posted and then deleted footage of fires in Dubai as debris from intercepted missiles smashed into two ritzy hotels in the city.

One influencer who wanted to remain anonymous shared a video of debris falling from the sky towards luxury hotel as he ran through the street.

But after initially agreeing to an interview, he told The Sun: “I’m a bit worried – can I go to jail for what I’ve posted?”

He then went silent, and deleted videos of the strike altogether.

What he posted next was even stranger.

The influencer went on to publish videos praising the UAE’s government, hopping on a trend that sucks up to the sheikhs.



The videos part of a bizarre trend where influencers show their lives in Dubai, captioned: “Aren’t you scared?”

The reels and TikToks then cut to clips of Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid al-Maktoum and other Emirati leaders, captioned: “No, I know who protects us.”

Some of the sycophantic reels may be genuine reaction from Dubai residents, many of whom claim to be living life as normal life despite the Iranian blitz on the nation.

Follow The Sun’s live blog for the latest updates on Iran

But as negative information about the UAE or criticising its government online is illegal, it’s hard to sift fact from fiction.



The UAE’s own defence boasts having intercepted 876 Iranian drones since the start of Iran‘s barrage, but admits that 65 drones and one missile have landed in its territory.

Many of the videos use footage of Emirati leaders first shared by the Dubai Media Office – the same state mouthpiece that has threatened to fine residents for telling the truth about chaos in the city.

One expert warned that “many people in Dubai are now extremely cautious about what they post online” after threats from the UAE government.

Insulting the UAE government can lead to fines of up to £200,000, deportation, or up to five years behind bars.

Radha Stirling, founder of Detained in Dubai, told The Sun: “It’s really frightening to have the police bullying people like that in times of crisis.”



She warned: “The UAE has extremely strict cybercrime laws that criminalise the publication of information considered damaging to the country’s reputation, security or public order.

“Authorities have recently reinforced those warnings.”

She worries that fear of recrimination has meant that “reality is effectively being filtered out of social media”.



She has been in contact with people who are afraid of sharing their true feelings about the attacks on Dubai, and told The Sun: “Some initially shared videos of explosions or interceptions in the sky, but quickly deleted or softened those posts once the warnings were circulated.

“People are genuinely worried that simply documenting what they are seeing or hearing could land them in serious legal trouble.”

There are also financial incentives to paint a perfect picture of the gulf state, which has repeatedly claimed to be one of the “safest places in the world”.

Desperate Dubai estate agents are motivated to keep the market moving – if people no longer want to move to the city, their business will break down.



Stocks in the UAE fell sharply when markets reopened this morning after an emergency pause – the state’s benchmark index dropped 4.7 per cent, its biggest drop in almost four years.

One influencer who posted and then deleted video of fires caused by burning debris told The Telegraph: “The Dubai authorities want to control the narrative, that’s for sure. There are strict rules about what you can say here.”

The Instagram influencer had filmed the burning rubble from outside of her apartment, but said she deleted her video and decided not to post any more “out of respect for the UAE’s rules”.

She added: “I was numb when I saw the flames. I didn’t know how to feel because at first, I didn’t know what I was looking at.

“But things have calmed down now, after a few days. People are even back on the beach. I feel quite safe.”



It comes as…

Stirling said: “Residents and influencers understand that their visa status, employment and even their liberty can be affected by what they publish, so many feel pressured to present a reassuring narrative rather than an accurate one.

“The result is that online content coming out of Dubai may not reflect what people are actually experiencing on the ground.

“There is a strong incentive to promote the message that everything is completely safe and normal, even when people are privately expressing concern.”

Fear is not just limited to influencers, Stirling said: “It’s not just social media types, everyone uses social media and everyone has been sharing videos, pictures and news.



She warned that cops could even crack down on information about attacks shared in private messages, adding: “It’s really frightening to have the police bullying people like that in times of crisis.

“You can be in violation of the law and face jail simply by liking or sharing someone else’s post, resharing a news article even.”

She added: “People have previously been at risk for posting negative content about ‘weather‘ and a strike from Iran is clearly more sensitive.”

Asked about the sycophantic sheikh-praising video trend, she said it began “because the attack didn’t seem so serious”.

But Stirling added: “Now that people are actually starting to panic, the trend seems almost a desperate attempt to preserve the reputation of Dubai as being safe.



“The truth is absolutely censored and many people are in tears over the threat. You have expats with families who don’t want their children staying in a country where there are explosions overhead and buildings on fire.

“They are getting multiple alerts to take shelter overnight and don’t know how prolonged the attacks are going to be. It’s a serious situation and there is no point in pretending it’s all okay.”



Some brave Brits are speaking out despite Emirati threats against free speech.

Bailey Nelson, 22, from Coventry, has lived in the Dubai for two years.

He has posted several updates on the situation in the UAE, including a video which shows debris streaking through Dubai’s night sky.

Bailey told The Sun: “The first one I saw personally falling from my apartment was in the direction of South Dubai, the media reports show it landed in Jebel Ali.

“I was just in a state of shock at first as it didn’t feel real.”



He moved to a hotel, but said he felt “the most safe you could possibly feel this close to an active war zone”.

Bailey is planning to return to the UK later this month for a planned trip, but said he would have no qualms about flying back to the UAE after that.

Asked about the trend of videos praising the UAE’s leaders, he said they were “very genuine”, adding that: “the Emirati leaders have it’s population of people in their best interest and always have done.”

While many may genuinely still feel safe in the Gulf state, other terrified Brits are trying to flee the region.

And others have admitted that they are afraid of sharing the truth.



One content creator told the Daily Mail: “People are scared to say anything negative.”

“Influencers have huge followings and they know they’re being watched.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Questions about free speech in the UAE make it hard to tell who genuinely feels safe in the region, and who is just putting on a brave face to keep their business in the Gulf state alive.

The Sun has contacted the Dubai Media Office for comment.

News.title: Oxford University professor composes song that is ‘scientifically proven’ to make rum taste even better
News.content:
AN OXFORD University professor has created a song which is ‘scientifically proven’ to make rum taste even better.

Sonic seasoning authority Professor Charles Spence applied cutting-edge research into how sound influences flavour perception to help inform a new bespoke track created by renowned Venezuelan pianist and composer Clara Rodríguez.





Studies into ‘cross modal perception’ show what we hear can prime the brain to notice certain tastes more strongly, meaning the right soundtrack can dial up sweetness, richness and warmth.

Rodríguez’s new track was designed to enhance the taste profile of Diplomático’s ultra-rare rum, Chancellor and follows the spirit’s butterscotch, vanilla and warming spice notes with accompanying aural textures that scientifically match the evolving tastes.

Together, they’re designed to nudge the brain into picking up on the rum’s sweeter, richer and more complex notes.

There are uplifting swells in the music to highlight brighter top notes, alongside slower, silkier passages to match the drink’s long, smooth finish.

Spence, who partnered with Diplomático to celebrate the launch of its limited-edition Chancellor rum, said: “Flavour is never just on the tongue, and the right notes can transform how these are perceived by our brains.

“In this piece of Venezuelan music, we translated the rum’s tasting notes into sound, so when people listen while they taste, the music gently nudges perception, making the rum feel richer, warmer, and more expressive.”

‘Sonic seasoning’ research has revealed how sound can enhance the way tastebuds perceive flavours – giving birth to a phenomenon where the brain links different audio frequencies to taste profiles.

Studies show specific pitches, tempos and tonal qualities can amplify sweetness, warmth and richness.



The four minute song, titled ‘The Chancellor’s Treasured Notes’, uses specific musical techniques to accentuate the nose, taste and finish of the Chancellor rum.

Rooted in the heritage shared by Rodríguez and Diplomático, the piece is a classical spin on Venezuelan folk music, connecting the origins behind the rum with rhythms and textures.

It starts with longer, sustained tones by the piano to accentuate the butterscotch while the bass brings lower notes to compliment the tobacco aromas, as it has been found within sensory studies that lower pitched sounds accentuate bitterness.

The cuatro and maracas are reminiscent of wind rustling through sugar cane plantations, establishing an evocative and sensory soundscape.

With the track being available to hear on YouTube.



Composer Clara Rodríguez, said: “Within this piece it was essential for me to connect with our shared Venezuelan origins, which is the cultural and emotional foundation of everything I compose.

“From the rhythms and textures to overall atmosphere, the spirit of Venezuela genuinely shines through.”

Elena Petrov, global brand director of the premium rum brand, said: “Diplomático Chancellor is an exceptional liquid that captures the very heart of our passion for craftsmanship.

“This collaboration with Carla Clara Rodríguez is a tribute to our shared Venezuelan soul, designed for those who understand that true luxury is found in the depth of the story behind the spirit – a story that can now be heard as clearly as it is tasted.”

News.title: Third man arrested over ‘spying for China’ is ex-Labour advisor – after MP’s husband & dad-of-3 held by cops
News.content:
‘SPY’ PROBE Third man arrested over ‘spying for China’ is ex-Labour advisor – after MP’s husband & dad-of-3 held by cops

AN ex-Labour advisor is among three people who were arrested on suspicion of spying for China.

Steve Jones, 68, is understood to be among the three men held by Counter Terror police on suspicion of assisting foreign intelligence.





Dad-of-three Matthew Aplin, 43, and Labour MP Joani Reid’s husband – David Taylor – were also seized by cops on Wednesday.

They were all released on bail a day later.

It is understood Jones was “ensconced” in Tony Blair‘s Labour and spent time at the heart of Number 10.

He was a special adviser to Hilary Armstrong, now Baroness Armstrong, when she was chief whip under Sir Tony Blair from 2001 until 2006.

An online profile describes how he “advised on all elements of political strategy development, legislative issues, backbench sentiment intelligence and media management”.

He also previously turned on the Welsh Labour government – calling it “toxic”.

Following the death of a minister who had been accused of misconduct in 2017, Jones rounded on the Welsh government and called out “pure poison” and “power plays”.



Taylor, Aplin and Jones were all held on suspicion of assisting a foreign intelligence service, contrary to section three of the National Security Act, 2023.

Aplin is understood to have close links to Labour, having worked for the party.

The former journalist, from Pontyclun, South Wales, was taken into custody at his home in Wales after raids were conducted across the country.

Plain-clothed police officers dressed in blue gloves and face masks were seen searching his detached home on a quiet cul de sac following the arrest.

Aplin began his career working as a journalist for the Western Mail before getting communications roles with the Welsh Government and Ofgem.



He later became head of communication and public affairs for the Public Services Ombudsman for Wales.

The former journalist works at the same Cardiff-based consultancy firm as Jones.

The public affairs firm Camlas says it has offices just “a stone’s throw from the Senedd”,

The managing director said Aplin has been suspended, meanwhile Jones’ profile has been deleted on its website.

“The police have not been in touch with us at Camlas and there are no links between Camlas and any Chinese companies or the Chinese government. We are a small Welsh public affairs agency,” they told The Telegraph.



Labour MP Joani Reid confirmed her husband was among those arrested on Wednesday.

She said in a statement that she had “never been to China” and insisted she is “not any sort of admirer or apologist for the Chinese Communist party’s dictatorship”.

The East Kilbride and Strathaven MP said: “I have never seen anything to make me suspect my husband has broken any law” and added: “I have never been to China.”



“I am not part of my husband’s business activities and neither I nor my children are part of this investigation.

“I have never been to China. I have never spoken on China or China related matters in the Commons. I have never asked a question on China-related matters.

“As far as I am aware I have never met any Chinese businesses whilst I have been an MP, any Chinese diplomats or government employees, nor raised any concern with ministers or anyone else on behalf of, even coincidentally, Chinese interests.

“I am a social democrat who believes in freedom of expression, free trade unions and free elections. I am not any sort of admirer or apologist for the Chinese Communist party’s dictatorship.”

Reid has served as the Member of Parliament for East Kilbride and Strathaven since July 2024.



Her husband is the head of programs at a think tank that develops engagement between Asia and Europe, and he previously worked for energy companies as a lobbyist.

He also worked as a government special adviser in the Wales Office under the previous New Labour government, it is understood.

British officials have made formal representations to their Chinese counterparts in both London and Beijing, MPs were told.

The bombshell arrests have prompted calls for a review of the decision to allow China’s new super-embassy in London – which many suggested could house a “spy factory”.

Spy chiefs have warned that they could not “wholly eliminate” risks from the embassy which conceals a chamber worryingly nearby to fibre-optic cables transmitting financial data .



The Met Police worked with cops in Wales and Scotland to search three addresses in London, East Kilbride and Cardiff on Wednesday.

Commander Helen Flanagan, head of Counter Terrorism Policing London, said: “We have seen a significant increase in our casework relating to national security in recent years and we continue to work extremely closely with our partners to help keep the country safe and take action to disrupt malign activity where we suspect it.

“Today’s arrests are part of a proactive investigation and while these are serious matters, we do not believe there to be any imminent or direct threat to the public relating to this.

“Our investigation continues and we thank the public for their ongoing support.”



Security minister Dan Jarvis has said there will be “severe consequences” if it is proven that China attempted to interfere with UK sovereign affairs.

He told MPs: “Let me be clear, if there is proven evidence of attempts by China to interfere with UK sovereign affairs, we will impose severe consequences and hold all actors involved to account.”

He added: “The Government is taking robust action to ensure the UK’s democratic institutions and processes are a hard target for this activity.”

“The National Security Act provides our intelligence agencies and law enforcement with the modern legal tools they need to deter, detect and disrupt the full range of state threats.”

“The action counter-terrorism police have been able to take this morning is an example that that legislation is working well.”



Mr Jarvis added: “We remain deeply concerned by an increasing pattern of covert activity from Chinese state-linked actors targeting UK democracy.

“This involves attempts to obtain information on UK policymaking and interfere with our sovereign affairs.”

It comes after a high-profile Chinese spy trial collapsed in September.

Chris Cash, a former head of a China policy group, and Christopher Berry were charged under the Official Secrets Act in April 2024.

They were accused of collecting and passing on information that could have aided an enemy. Both men have denied any wrongdoing.



The prosecution collapsed when barrister Tom Little KC told the Old Bailey: “We simply cannot continue to prosecute this case.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

In a letter to MPs, Stephen Parkinson said: “Notwithstanding the fact that further witness statements were provided, none of these stated that at the time of the offence China represented a threat to national security, and by late August 2025 it was realised that this evidence would not be forthcoming.”

The Prime Minister insisted the blame lay with the former Tory government for failing to formally label Beijing a threat, leaving Labour unable to do so retrospectively.

News.title: Popular face serums urgently recalled over ‘serious chemical risk’ as shoppers warned ‘stop using it immediately’
News.content:
TWO well-loved face creams have been recalled over concerns some ingredients that could affect the health of an unborn child.

The Office for Product Safety and Standards (OPSS) urgently recalled the two face creams because they posed “serious chemical risk.”





Creams affected are the Glow & Lovely Hydraglow Rose Enrich Serum Cream 50g and Glow & Lovely Advanced Multi Vitamin Serum in Cream.

OPSS said the latter was recalled because it could contain the chemical compound Butylphenyl methylpropional (BMHCA).

The chemical is not allowed in cosmetic products in the UK because it could “harm the reproductive system or the health of the unborn child and may cause skin sensitisation,” per the OPSS.



The Rose Enrich Serum was recalled because it posed a “serious chemical risk as the ingredients list had incorrect ingredients listed and had not specified fragrance allergens.” It also did not comply with cosmetic regulations.

“Consumers who are sensitive to certain allergens are not informed and may experience an adverse effect.”

For both creams, the OPSS advised for users to “stop using this product immediately and contact the distributor to request redress.”

The serums were largely available online but the OPSS confirmed they have been completely removed from sale by distributor Lajawab Foods Limited.



The latest recall comes just hours after superstar singer Rihanna’s perfume was also recalled over a chemical that could pose a danger for users.

UK retailer Savers issued the warning, which applies to two products from the singer’s 15ml line.

The following product names and barcodes are included in the product recall:

Rihanna Kiss 15ml EDP, barcode: 0608940572252

Rihanna Riri 15ml ECP, barcode: 0608940560389

The recall, which was officially issued on Tuesday, March 3, detailed the possible inclusion of hydroxyisohexyl 3-cyclohexene carboxaldehyde and butylphenyl methylpropional in the fragrances.

Both of these chemicals are prohibited from sale in the UK and the EU.



The chemicals, also known as Lyral and Lilial, were banned in December 2022 because their use has been associated with harm to the reproductive system, as well as harming unborn babies.

Customers have been advised to return any of the affected products to their local Savers store for a full refund.

News.title: Moment Arbroath Asda security guard confronts shoplifter before tragic death
News.content:
Shocking bodycam footage shows the moment an Asda security guard was violently assaulted by a shoplifter before he collapsed and died.

Natasha Smith, 38, launched the horrific attack on former Royal Marine Alun Harris-Richards, 61, while he was working a shift at the supermarket in Arbroath, Angus, on June 30, 2024. Her outburst came after Mr Harris-Richards stopped her from leaving the store with stolen alcohol.

This is a breaking news story.

News.title: Sirens blare at UK military base in Cyprus amid possible missile security alert
News.content:
Sirens blare at UK military base in Cyprus amid possible missile security alert

Defence Secretary was meeting government leaders in Cyprus as sirens sounded at the RAF Akrotiri base on the island just five days after an Iranian drone landed just short of the runway

View 4 Images The RAF base in Cyprus was targeted by an Iranian-made drone earlier this week (Image: Getty Images)

A UK military base on Cyprus was the centre of a security alert today as the Defence Secretary visited the island.

‌



John Healey was meeting Cypriot Government leaders as the sirens sounded and an aircraft took off from the Akrotiri base, which is near Limassol. The alert came just five days after an Iranian drone landed just short of the runway at the base used by around 4,000 RAF and other UK military personnel, and 7,000 civilians and their family members.

‌



The alarm sounded just before 12.45 pm local time and was heard for several minutes as a jet took to the skies. It is understood radar picked up a possible missile approximately 15 minutes from the base.

‌



View 4 Images Defence Secretary John Healey is visiting Cyprus after the RAF base was hit by a missile (Image: Getty Images)

Azerbaijan airport attack LIVE: Iranian drones cause massive explosion READ MORE :

Latest Foreign Office travel advice - new 13 countries updated in last 24 hours READ MORE :

The siren stopped about five to 10 minutes after it had originally sounded. Experts warn that Cyprus remains a key target for Iran because it is within range of its drone missiles.

The island remains a popular destination for Brits with around 1.2 million visitors annually. The Shahed drone used in Sunday's attack cannot carry a large warhead. While it could kill an individual, it would be unlikely to damage large buildings.

‌



The service personnel and their families living on RAF Akrotiri had 10 minutes to find cover. Armed services members with families have now been evacuated and nearby schools are closed. The drone missile landed about 800m from the US side of the base. RAF Typhoons scrambled in a bid to shoot them down.

View 4 Images A Fighter Jet prepares for landing at the U.K.'s RAF Akrotiri air base after it was hit by a drone strike near Limassol (Image: AP)

‌



Just 11 minutes later, residents received mobile phone alerts telling them to take cover under “substantial, solid furniture." It is believed the missiles were fired from Lebanon or Iraq and evaded detection systems by skimming close to the Mediterranean Sea, meaning they were not detected until they were 20 minutes from the target.

Professor Peter Lee, who specialises in war ethics and drone warfare at the University of Portsmouth, said Iran has been describing the UK as an enemy and target for some time. He added: "The UK is probably the closest European ally of the United States. Iran has already identified the UK as a potential enemy and its missiles will not reach London. Cyprus is the nearest collection of Brits at the military base."

RAF Akrotiri is a major British military presence on the Mediterranean island of Cyprus. It sits inside the UK's Western Sovereign Base Area, British-controlled territory overseas, and is one of Britain's most important strategic sites abroad.

Article continues below

View 4 Images RAF Akotiri sits inside the UK's Western Soverign Base Area and is one of Britain's most strategic sites abroad (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Built in the 1950s, the base has played a role in major global events for decades, from the Suez Crisis to operations in Iraq, Syria and Libya. More recently, RAF jets based there have carried out missions against ISIS and Houthi targets in Yemen.

Because of its location, RAF Akrotiri is a key launch point for British operations in the Middle East. It is also used as a staging and transit base during evacuations and international crises.

News.title: Shoplifter jailed after Arbroath Asda security guard dies following fight
News.content:
A shoplifter got into a fight with an Asda security guard who later died.

Alun Harris-Richards, 61, who worked as a security guard for the Asda superstore in Arbroath, suffered a heart attack and fell to the ground where he struck his head and died after confronting Natasha Smith.

Smith, 38, was prevented from leaving the store at Westbury Retail Park on suspicion she had stolen alcohol.

On Thursday, Smith was jailed for five years and four months, after pleading guilty to culpable homicide. She also pleaded guilty to a charge involving the assault of another Asda employee on June 30, 2024.

During the altercation, the court heard Smith struggled with Mr-Harris Richards and another member of staff. As Smith pulled at the bag containing the stolen booze, which was being held by Mr Harris-Richards, she flew into a rage, making threatening and abusive remarks to him as seen in shocking bodycam footage.

Image: Police Scotland) Police Scotland)

At that point, Mr Harris-Richards, a former Royal Marine, struggled to breathe and fell to the floor. Despite the best efforts of paramedics as well as staff and customers, who gave him CPR, he died at the scene.

The court heard how he "suffered such physical and emotional stress that he then and there sustained a heart attack, fell to the ground, struck his head and died".

A post-mortem later revealed he had undiagnosed heart disease and died of a heart attack.

Meanwhile, another member of staff continued to struggle with Smith, who subjected her to a violent attack.

Moira Orr, who leads on homicide for the Crown Office and Procurator Fiscal Service (COPFS), said: “This was an unprovoked attack on a man who was simply doing his job.

“Natasha Smith’s actions resulted in tragic consequences and robbed a much-loved family man of his life.

“She will now serve a prison sentence for her crime.

“This conviction serves as a warning to others that we take crimes of this nature extremely seriously and will use all the tools available to us to seek justice.

“Our thoughts lie with Mr Harris-Richards’ family and friends. We are sorry for their loss.”

News.title: Mum in tears after realising what husband gave son to wash hair with
News.content:
Mum in tears after realising what husband gave son to wash hair with

A mother was in floods of tears after realising what product her husband gave their son to wash his hair with in the shower, after raiding her toiletry stash...

View Image Her son was using something he shouldn't have on his hair (Image: Shared Content Unit)

A mum was in hysterical tears after realising what product her husband gave their son to wash his hair with when she wasn't around. For people that are not used to the huge amount of feminine products women use in their shower and beauty routines, it can be overwhelming to know what their uses are.

‌



This was true for one dad who "helped" his son when he ran out of shampoo for his hair. He found a bottle from his wife's beauty stash, and without reading what it was for, gave it to his son.

‌



He said: "You can use mummy's until she gets you a new one." The first the wife, who has 1.3 million followers on TikTok posting under the username @imtheproblem, heard of the problem was when her son asked if she could smell his hair.

‌



She said: "I sniffed his head. He said, 'I don't like that new shampoo... I'm using that orange bottle'.

"I'm thinking about all of my shampoo bottles and I'm like, OK, the orange one, it's organic. It's not as perfumed as Herbal Essences, but your hair's still clean.

"And then yesterday he's having a shower and he's forgotten a towel. So I take him in a towel and immediately my eyes go towards the orange bottle that he's been washing his hair with for the last few days.

‌



"He's been washing his hair with Femfresh. I was right, it's definitely less perfumed than Herbal Essences, but it's definitely not shampoo.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

"So I obviously find this hilarious. I go outside the room laughing. My daughter says, why are you laughing? I explained to her why I'm laughing.

‌



"And she says, well, serves him right because you did say he needs to start reading more at home, which makes me laugh even more. I go downstairs and I explain to my husband why I'm laughing.

"He said, I gave that to him. So they've both learned a valuable lesson to read the labels of things and stop going through my stuff."

Hundreds of other people commented on the video to share their hilarious stories of using the wrong products.

Article continues below

One person said: "I was using wipes to remove my make up everyday once and told people how amazing they were and my skin was so soft. They asked me what they were. It turned out to be Femfresh wipes, I tell you my skin was amazing."

A second said: "My brother used to live with me and I realised my Femfresh was going down very rapidly for some reason. Found out my brother was using it to wash himself as he thought it was body wash haha his face when I told him what it was."

News.title: Nigel Farage makes jibe at heckler who interrupted speech - 'Oh sorry darling!'
News.content:
Nigel Farage has been mocked a heckler for "not having a job" after being interrupted during a press conference in Wales.

The Reform UK leader lashed out after a young woman spoke out during Reform’s Welsh manifesto launch, ahead of Senedd elections in May.

Responding, he said: "Oh sorry darling are you having a rough morning? Did you get out on the wrong side of bed? It's alright, you always get the odd screamer at these events. Go back to work." As Reform UK members laughed and cheered, he added: "I did say go back to work, but of course she hasn't got a job has she".

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Image: PA) PA)

The clash came as Mr Farage told Welsh members of this party that Britain finds itself “humiliated”, with its reputation “frankly going down the drain” after failing to join US and Israeli attacks on Iran.

He said: “If there was one big justifiable reason why we should have supported the Americans and supported the Israelis on day one, if this war stops Iran from getting a nuclear bomb, it will have been worth it. What did our prime minister do? He said to the Americans ‘you cannot use military bases in the United Kingdom and you can’t use Diego Garcia’.

“Then he says ‘we will put fighter jets in the sky in a defensive capacity’. And now we learn that Ed Miliband led the sort of left-wing revolt in Cabinet and we find ourselves humiliated. What was for centuries the greatest naval nation on Earth is now unable, for up to a fortnight, to send a Type 45 to Cyprus to defend British sovereign territory.”

It comes as a poll suggested more than half of Reform UK members think non-white British citizens born abroad should be forcibly removed or encouraged to leave - compared to 24% if the citizens are white. In its annual report, HOPE not Hate (HNH) said racial backgrounds “play a major factor in determining Reform UK members’ views on immigration”.

News.title: King Charles' 'strategic reason' for snubbing Andrew despite being a few miles apart
News.content:
King Charles' 'strategic reason' for snubbing Andrew despite being a few miles apart

The monarch avoided seeing his disgraced younger brother of the weekend while on his Norfolk estate, and now a former royal aide has revealed why the King cannot see Andrew in the wake of his arrest

View 4 Images Andrew with his older brother King Charles (Image: Getty)

King Charles cannot meet with his disgraced brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor over fears it would “escalate” his situation, according to a former royal aide. Over the weekend, the monarch travelled to his Norfolk estate and despite being just miles away from the former prince’s temporary home of Wood Farm, did not visit his younger brother.

On Sunday, Charles was pictured walking to St Mary Magdalene Church at Sandringham for a traditional morning service. Wearing a camel coloured overcoat and holding a large umbrella, the King was seen shaking hands with a member of the clergy as he walked the short distance from Sandringham House.

Sarah Ferguson's pal reveals her true state of mind as she continues to hide READ MORE :

Meghan Markle insider gives surprise As Ever update as sales figures revealed READ MORE :

View 4 Images King Charles didn't see Andrew when he was at Sandringham over the weekend (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

The King was also seen driving around his estate on Saturday and at one point was less than two miles away from the former Duke of York's current home of Wood Farm, where he is living while his more permanent lodgings of Marsh Farm are undergoing renovations.

Former Press Secretary to Queen Elizabeth II, Ailsa Anderson, told The Sun that Charles cannot meet with his brother, even if he wanted to, for one major reason.

She told the publication: “I think the story at the moment has not escalated in the last week since the arrest and sort of all the furore after that. I think if the King went to see him, somebody would take a shot. It would escalate the story and it would bring it back to the surface again.

“So, yes, the king is probably communicating [with Andrew] on the telephone. But I think he’s right not to go and see him because I just think the story would suddenly resurface and escalate and all the things that the palace doesn't want.”

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

View 4 Images The King attended a church service and was just miles from Andrew's Wood Farm home (Image: PA)

Royal biographer Phil Dampier offered an alternate view, saying he would be “surprised” if Charles and Andrew didn’t meet in person given their close proximity on the Sandringham estate.

He said: “I think probably he’s talking to him behind the scenes and saying, ‘look, I’m sorry, I’ve had to do this for the sake of the Monarchy'. At the end of the day he is his brother, and I’m sure he feels for him to some extent.”

Andrew has received two visitors in the last week: the Reverend Canon Paul Williams, who visited the former prince just hours before he met with Charles on Sunday morning, and Lord Chamberlain of the Royal household Richard Benyon, the most senior member of the household, who spoke with Andrew at Wood Farm on Monday.

Since Andrew’s arrest, which took place on his 66th birthday, strict rules have been imposed on the former prince, including that he has been told he is not allowed to go out horse riding, something he did often while living at the much grander Royal Lodge on the Windsor estate, as it's considered a "bad look" in the wake of the police probe into him.

View 4 Images Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was arrested last month (Image: PA)

Article continues below

According to the i newspaper, it is claimed further rules have been imposed upon Mountbatten-Windsor - and that he cannot leave the Sandringham estate without permission and a chaperone. It is further claimed that any visitors to his home must also be approved in advance.

Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was arrested on February 19 on suspicion of misconduct in public office, with the arrest linked to his time as UK trade envoy. The disgraced royal spent 11 hours in custody at a Norfolk police station, before being released under investigation.

News.title: 'I've got the biggest boobs in Britain - getting out of bed takes me hours'
News.content:
'I've got the biggest boobs in Britain - getting out of bed takes me hours'

A mum says her 36M breasts cause crippling pain and make everyday tasks virtually impossible. She's heartbroken that she can't even pick her young daughter up

View 3 Images The mum is raising money for a breast reduction (Image: Katielee Arrowsmith / SWNS)

A woman claiming to have the 'biggest boobs in Britain' says her breasts are heavy that getting out of bed each morning is nearly impossible.

‌



Melissa Ashcroft, 30, has endured serious back pain since puberty and can't even lift up her daughter. She initially attempted to secure a breast reduction at 20 but claims her GP advised waiting until after having children and breastfeeding.

‌



However, following the birth of her two children, thee mum, from Blairgowrie, Scotland, was told last year that her BMI of 35 is too high and must drop to 27 to be eligible for NHS surgery. "I had my son at 23 and my daughter is eight months old, and they've now said my BMI is too high," she explained. "I was told to get down to 11st. The anaesthetic won't work as well and I'm more at risk of blood clots and infections."

‌



Living in constant pain and discomfort, Melissa says proper exercise is impossible - and she's now raising £8,000 through crowdfunding for private treatment to reduce her breasts, which tip the scales at two and a half stone.

View 3 Images Melissa lives in constant pain (Image: Katielee Arrowsmith / SWNS)

"The doctors told me that because I've just had my baby my hormones are all over the place and my weight will gradually start coming back down," the mum explained last year. "It has come done a bit, but how am I supposed to do it? I can't go to the gym, it's too embarrassing and I can't go for a run because of the pain.

‌



"I think I've got some of the biggest boobs in Britain. I'm a size 36M," Melissa revealed, adding that bras in her size cost £82. "I can't pick up my daughter and that's heartbreaking. I want to be able to play with her and pick her up. If I'm cleaning, I have to take a break until I'm out of pain. In the morning, I can't do anything until I've done my back stretches.

"I've got a picture of me when I'm 18 when I was a size 6 or an 8 and it looks like I paid to have these fake boobs," she said. At one stage, she stepped on the scales and asked her husband to support her breasts, which resulted in two-and-a-half stone 'coming off' her weight.

View 3 Images The mum says her breasts weigh two and a half stone (Image: Melissa Ashcroft / SWNS)

‌



Melissa says she's been advised to consider weight loss injections, but two relatives have experienced serious adverse reactions to them - one required her gallstones removed and one was left feeling nauseous after consuming anything - and it's not something she's willing to try.

Ironically, Melissa says she was tormented at school for being flat-chested before she reached puberty. But both her mum and nan have large breasts so she believes it's a 'hereditary thing'.

"One minute I was getting bullied for having a flat chest, then I'd get bullied saying I had tea towels down my bra," she said. "It wasn't nice at school - it was everyday. Physically, it was causing pain immediately."

Article continues below

The mum said that if she can gather the funds for the operation it would be "all my Christmases in one day", adding: "It would be a complete life changer - I'd be able to play with my kids at the park and pick up my daughter." You can visit her GoFundMe page here.

News.title: Chris Wheeler dead: Great British Menu chef passes away as friends pay tribute
News.content:
A BBC chef who starred in Great British Menu has sadly died. Chris Wheeler appeared on the show in 2016, and famously ran the London Marathon flipping a pancake. His cause of death is currently unknown.

TV chef Jean-Christophe Novelli paid tribute to his former colleague, praising him as an “extraordinary” man and chef. He wrote: “It is with the heaviest of hearts that I say goodbye to my dear friend, Chris Wheeler. His loss is a devastating blow to the industry and to humanity.

“Chris was an extraordinary chef, but even more extraordinary as a man – warm, generous, steady, and endlessly kind. I invited Chris to come and work for me when he was a 16-year-old college student.

Image: Bucks Advertiser) Bucks Advertiser)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

“For more than 30 years he was more than a friend to me – he was my rock. I was always so proud to see him enjoy the success he deserved and I will miss him forever.”

Fans were quick to send their condolences. One wrote: "Beautiful & soulful words JC & a fitting tribute to a truly remarkable chef. He’ll be hugely missed but never forgotten." Another said: "A true gentleman and fierce grafter RIP Chris."

Dorset born Chris trained at Bournemouth catering college then started his career in a Michelin kitchen in France. Jean-Christophe Novelli recognised his talents and over a ten year period he worked at Le Provence, Gordleton Mill and the Four Seasons.

Talking about his work at Le Provence, Chris told The Caterer in 2010: "Every Monday we'd have the likes of Raymond, Marco - my heroes! - coming down to see us. I still have the photos." He added: "Getting the first Michelin star was a huge high. We were so excited we jumped in the river - it was January so it was quite cold!"

In 2003, he left the Novelli empire to become head chef at Humphry’s restaurant in Stoke Park which has since won three AA rosettes. The chef admitted he wanted to be a zoo-keeper but changed his mind after his nine-month catering work placement in France. "Catering was my second choice," he said. "I suppose it's nearly the same though - feeding animals!"

Chris was originally from Swanage in Dorset and was highly competitive and not just in the kitchen. He regularly raised money for charity running half or full marathons - sometimes carrying a 5kg stockpot.

In 2006, he ran the London Marathon while dressed as a chef, flipping a pancake for the entire race, finishing in six hours and 50 minutes. He also baked the cake that starred in the 2004 Daniel Craig film Layer Cake.

Boxing.title: Eddie Hearn claims Conor Benn offered him money to keep quiet over his controversial decision to defect to bitter rival Dana White's Zuffa Boxing
Boxing.content:
Eddie Hearn has claimed that Conor Benn offered him money to avoid speaking negatively about their split after the British boxer signed a blockbuster deal with Zuffa Boxing.

Benn's decision to leave Hearn's Matchroom stable after a decade together came as a surprise within the industry, with the 29-year-old agreeing terms on what has been widely reported as a $15million one-fight deal with the new promotional venture headed up by Dana White.

Hearn, who guided Benn from his professional debut at 19 and oversaw his rise to world-level contention, admitted he was 'devastated' by the departure. He also revealed he was given just three days to match the offer - which he felt was 'completely unreasonable commercially'.

Despite Benn publicly insisting he would still like a relationship with his former promoter and even suggesting there could be a place for him within his team, Hearn says part of the exit discussions left him stunned and disappointed in his former star.

Speaking to iFL TV about the way in which Benn handled the situation, Hearn said: 'There was something else which was, "We'll give you a few quid if you don't say anything bad", kind of thing.

'And I was like, What? "We still want to work with you and here's a few quid".

Hearn has claimed Benn offered him money to avoid speaking negatively about their split

Benn announced he had jumped ship to Dana White's promotional company Zuffa Boxing

'And I'm just like… so I've received it and it's gone to Frank Smith and Shaun Palmer, who we're kind of the main operators within the business.

'And we're all then straight on like, "Have you seen that? Are they f***ing having a laugh?" Everyone was just completely shocked.'

Hearn also expressed frustration at how the communication was handled, revealing he first learned of developments formally through Benn's legal team rather than directly from the fighter.

He said: 'I text Conor maybe four or five days later and said, "I really think we should have a call. For everything that I've done for you, I don't think it's fair that I should have to deal with a letter from your lawyer. Let's just have a chat".

'Basically, man to man. And he said, "I heard you were quite emotional on the call. I think we should just let it all die down and talk another time".

'I'm like, "f*** me". And that was probably the moment where I just thought I cannot believe on the believe.'

While reiterating that he respects any fighter's right to maximise their earning potential, Hearn insisted his issue was not financial.

'To be honest, I think people get it a little bit mixed up between a fighter shouldn't be denied an opportunity to make a load of money for their family and all this kind of stuff,' he said.

Benn's decision to leave Hearn's Matchroom stable after a decade together came as a surprise

Conor Benn will fight Regis Prograis on April 11 on the same card as the returning Tyson Fury

'I couldn't agree more. This is not about that. This is just the way that you act to someone that has done so much for you and has basically saved your career.

'And if you listen to the Conor Benn story, I don't want to say saved his life, but the people who were there for him like that – which you can count on one hand – played a massive part in that.

'So to forget them or to treat them with disloyalty for me is unforgivable.'

Benn's next bout is scheduled for April 11 in a 150lb catchweight clash against former 140lb world champion Regis Prograis.

He is coming off two high-profile contests with Chris Eubank Jr and remains highly ranked at welterweight as he prepares to begin the next chapter of his career under new promotional leadership.

Boxing.title: Inside the incredible plans to transform the Pyramids of Giza into a heavyweight boxing venue for Oleksandr Usyk's historic fight
Boxing.content:
Oleksandr Usyk will swap packed stadiums for one of the most extraordinary settings sport has ever seen when he takes on former kickboxing champion Rico Verhoeven on May 23.

The two men are set to clash in front of the Pyramids of Giza with the WBC heavyweight world title on the line after Mauricio Sulaiman confirmed the green and gold belt will be at stake.

The WBC president has granted Usyk special permission to make a voluntary defence of his title before fulfilling his mandatory obligation, a bout against interim champion Agit Kabayel.

For Usyk, May's event will represent a slightly unconventional chapter in a career defined by taking on the biggest challenges in the most hostile environments.

Verhoeven, meanwhile, will dip his toe into boxing's ultimate proving ground and his first outing in a professional boxing ring for a decade after years of kickboxing dominance.

For organisers, it will be more than just a fight. It will be a global event, designed to merge elite combat sport with one of the most iconic backdrops on Earth.

Oleksandr Usyk will swap packed stadiums for one of the most extraordinary settings sport has ever seen when he takes on former kickboxing champion Rico Verhoeven on May 23

The two men are set to clash in front of the Pyramids of Giza (Verhoeven pictured above)

When Tiesto took to the decks on the Pyramids of Giza last December, the ancient plateau was transformed into something resembling a sci-fi movie set.

Lasers sliced through the sky, vast LED screens rose from the sand, and the pyramids themselves were illuminated like the backdrop to a Hollywood movie.

Yet for all the spectacle, what mattered most was what you couldn't see.

Behind the scenes, the entire operation ran like a military exercise. The stage was completely temporary - no digging, no drilling, no permanent fixings.

Every truss, lighting tower and screen was carefully weighted and balanced to ensure the desert surface (and the buried archaeology beneath it) remained untouched.

It's believed a fight involving Usyk would follow exactly the same blueprint: a raised, freestanding ring platform constructed entirely above ground and dismantled within hours of the final bell.

One obvious concern is noise. Heavyweight boxing is not exactly subtle.

But during Tiesto's show, engineers developed a 'heritage-safe' sound system that directed bass frequencies away from the pyramids.

When Tiesto took to the decks on the Pyramids of Giza last December, the ancient plateau was transformed into something resembling a sci-fi movie set

The WBC president has granted Usyk special permission to make a voluntary defence before fulfilling his mandatory obligation, a bout that must come against Agit Kabayel

For Usyk, May's event will represent a slightly unconventional chapter in a career defined by taking on the biggest challenges in the most hostile environments

Real-time vibration sensors monitored the limestone structures throughout the night to ensure sound levels stayed within strict archaeological limits.

A boxing fight would produce nothing like the sustained low-frequency rumble of a three-hour electronic set. Even so, vibration monitoring would almost certainly be in place again as an added safeguard.

The logistics would be just as tightly controlled. For Tiesto's event, guests entered via the Grand Egyptian Museum before being transported by dedicated shuttle to the secured concert zone on the plateau. A fight night would be expected to follow a similarly high-end access plan.

The concert footprint included a vast main arena space, VIP table sections, hospitality and brand activation zones, plus food and beverage areas spread across the sand.

For boxing, that layout could easily translate into a ringside enclosure carved into the desert, elevated hospitality decks and dramatic broadcast lighting tailored for a global pay-per-view audience.

Security, too, was uncompromising. Mandatory bag checks, controlled access points and a strict no re-entry policy were enforced throughout the concert - measures that would likely be even tighter for a world-title bout staged in the shadow of the last surviving wonder of the ancient world.

Boxing.title: Anthony Joshua takes a shot at Conor Benn in Instagram post as Brit shows his support for long-time promoter Eddie Hearn after former stablemate left Matchroom for rival Zuffa Boxing
Boxing.content:
Anthony Joshua takes a shot at Conor Benn in Instagram post as Brit shows his support for long-time promoter Eddie Hearn after former stablemate left Matchroom for rival Zuffa Boxing

Anthony Joshua has waded into the ongoing feud between Conor Benn and Eddie Hearn, lending his support to the promoter.

Benn, son of British legend Nigel, sensationally announced last month that he was ditching Hearn's promotional team Matchroom Boxing after a decade-long relationship - leaving for his rival Dana White's outfit Zuffa Boxing instead.

Hearn has since spoken out about that decision, revealing he was left 'devastated' by Benn having guided the British boxer from his 2016 debut to headlining cards and sticking by him after failing two drug tests in 2022.

And on Tuesday another long-term Matchroom Boxing fighter in Joshua showed his support to Hearn.

Uploading to his Instagram story, the former two-time heavyweight world champion shared a picture of him and Hearn dressed sharply.

Accompanied with that was a caption which read: 'AJ: Did you bring the fire power Eddie?

Anthony Joshua (left) took to Instagram on Tuesday to lend his support for Eddie Hearn (right) after Conor Benn left his Matchroom Boxing for rivals Zuffa Boxing last month

Benn (top) stunned Hearn when he decided to end their 10-year working relationship
